rules.name: Grid System
rules.description: Use a 12-column grid for flexible and consistent layouts.
rules.application: Constrain content to a max-width container with column-based sizing.
rules.example: 1200px container with 12 columns, 24px gutters, sidebar spanning 3 columns.
rules.anti_pattern: Absolute positioning of elements without any grid alignment.

rules.name: Content Hierarchy Zones
rules.description: Divide the viewport into clear zones: navigation, content, and actions.
rules.application: Keep navigation at the top or side, content in the center, actions contextual.
rules.example: Top nav bar, left sidebar for navigation, main content area, right panel for details.
rules.anti_pattern: Mixing navigation, content, and actions together in one undifferentiated area.

rules.name: Responsive Breakpoints
rules.description: Design for key breakpoints: mobile (375), tablet (768), desktop (1024+).
rules.application: Stack layouts vertically on mobile, side-by-side on desktop.
rules.example: Two-column layout on desktop collapsing to single column on mobile.
rules.anti_pattern: Only designing for desktop and ignoring mobile viewport entirely.

rules.name: Z-Pattern and F-Pattern
rules.description: Arrange content following natural eye scanning patterns.
rules.application: Place key information along the Z or F path for Western reading patterns.
rules.example: Logo top-left, nav top-right, hero content center-left, CTA center-right.
rules.anti_pattern: Important content placed bottom-right where eyes rarely reach first.

rules.name: Container Queries
rules.description: Design components that adapt to their container, not just the viewport.
rules.application: Cards should look good in a sidebar (narrow) or main content (wide).
rules.example: A card component that stacks vertically when narrow, goes horizontal when wide.
rules.anti_pattern: Components that only work at one specific width and break elsewhere.

rules.name: Sticky Elements
rules.description: Use sticky positioning for persistently needed elements like navigation.
rules.application: Stick navigation headers and action bars while scrolling content.
rules.example: A top navigation bar that stays visible when scrolling down the page.
rules.anti_pattern: Making everything sticky, reducing the available content area significantly.

rules.name: Overflow Handling
rules.description: Plan for content overflow: truncation, scrolling, or expansion.
rules.application: Define how long text, many items, or large images behave in containers.
rules.example: Long usernames truncated with ellipsis in a fixed-width sidebar.
rules.anti_pattern: Text overflowing its container and overlapping adjacent elements.
